zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Jackson於1591年2月7日之前出生在英國米德萊可瓦波三時，她的父親Richard Jackson30歲，母親Elizabeth Coffin-Jackson27歲。她於1627年9月19日與Samuel Pettypool Poole在英國英格蘭米德萊可斯特普尼聖 登斯坦 安德 哥吉拉 沙智 邱奇結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1667年在英國英格蘭米德萊可斯特普尼聖 登斯坦 安德 哥吉拉 沙智 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Jackson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and northern Irish: patronymic from Jack . In North America, this surname has absorbed other patronymics beginning with J- in various European languages, in particular those derived from equivalents or short forms and other derivatives of the personal name Jacob , e.g. Norwegian Jacobsen or Jakobsen and, in some cases, Slovenian Jakše (from a derivative of the personal name Jakob ). This surname is also very common among African Americans (see also 2 below).

African American: from the personal name Jackson (or Andrew Jackson), adopted in honor of Andrew Jackson, the 7th president of the US; or adoption of the surname in 1 above, in many cases probably for the same reason.

History: This extremely common British name was brought over by numerous different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. One forebear was the father and namesake of the seventh US president, Andrew Jackson, who migrated to SC from Carrickfergus in the north of Ireland in 1765. The Confederate General Thomas ‘Stonewall’ Jackson came from VA, where his great-grandfather John, likewise of Scotch–Irish stock, had settled after emigrating to America in 1748.

variants.name: Jack
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English and Older Scots personal name Jak, Jakke, Jagge, Jake, Jeke, Jegge, a Picard-Flemish denasalized form of Old Picard and Middle Dutch Janke, a pet form of Jan (see John ). It was introduced by Flemings and Picards into Norman and Anglo-Norman usage, whence it became a common English and Scottish pet form of John. Although the surname is mainly Scottish in distribution, it also occurs in England, though the more common form there is Jackson .

English: occasionally perhaps from a Middle English borrowing of the Old French personal name Jacque(s) (James). However, it is uncertain whether English Jack was ever used as an alternative to James.
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name Jack (see 1 above) as a surname.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Jackson was born before 7 February 1591, in Wapping, Middlesex, England, her father, Richard Jackson, was 30 and her mother, Elizabeth Coffin-Jackson, was 27. She married Samuel Pettypool Poole on 19 September 1627, in Saint Dunstan and All Saints Church, Stepney, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. She died in 1667, in Saint Dunstan and All Saints Church, Stepney, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom.

ja.Paignton,,Devon,England: イギリスイギリスデボントルバイペイグントン
ja.lifeSummary: Alice Jacksonが1591年2月7日以前にイギリスミッドレセックスワップイングで生まれた時，父Richard Jacksonは30歳，母Elizabeth Coffin-Jacksonは27歳であった。1627年9月19日にイギリスイギリスミッドレセックスステプニーサイント ダンスタン アンド アル サインツ チャーチでSamuel Pettypool Pooleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1667年にイギリスイギリスミッドレセックスステプニーサイント ダンスタン アンド アル サインツ チャーチで死去した。

ko.St Dunstan and All Saints, Stepney, Midd: 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 스테p니 세인트 던스탠 앤드 앨 세인츠 c후르치
ko.St. Martin Orgar And Saint Clement Eastc: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 클레멘트 이스트체아프 위스 스트 마틴 르가
ko.St Dunstan, Stepney, Middlesex, England: 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 스테p니 세인트 던스탠 앤드 앨 세인츠 c후르치
ko.Saint Dunstan,Stepney,London,England: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스테p니 세인트 던스탠 앤드 앨 세인츠 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Alice Jackson(이)가 1591년 2월 7일 이전 영국 미들레세스 왑핀그에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Jackson(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Coffin-Jackson(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1627년 9월 19일 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 스테p니 세인트 던스탠 앤드 앨 세인츠 c후르치에서 Samuel Pettypool Poole(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1667년 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 스테p니 세인트 던스탠 앤드 앨 세인츠 c후르치에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Jackson nasceu antes 7 de fevereiro de 1591, em Wapping, Middlesex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Jackson, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Coffin-Jackson, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Samuel Pettypool Poole em 19 de setembro de 1627, em Saint Dunstan and All Saints Church, Stepney, Middlesex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1667, em Saint Dunstan and All Saints Church, Stepney, Middlesex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice Jackson родилась перед 7 февраль 1591 г. в местности ваппинг, миддлесекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Jackson было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Coffin-Jackson, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Samuel Pettypool Poole 19 сентября 1627 г. в местности сеинт дунстань анд алл сеинц чурч, степни, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла в 1667 г. в местности сеинт дунстань анд алл сеинц чурч, степни, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alice Jackson nació antes de 7 de febrero de 1591, en Wapping, Middlesex, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Jackson, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Coffin-Jackson, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Samuel Pettypool Poole el 19 de septiembre de 1627, en Saint Dunstan and All Saints Church, Stepney, Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió en 1667, en Saint Dunstan and All Saints Church, Stepney, Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
